semanticCore: Laziness, sluggishness, slow state, or inactive
quranicRelevance: Hypocrites standing up for prayer lazily (Kusala - 'qamoo kusala', 'wa-la ya'toona as-salata illa wahum kusala').

keyDerivations.meaning: Lazily, sluggishly, or in a state of inactivity

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ يُرَاءُونَ النَّاسَ (4:142): And when they stand up for prayer, they stand up lazily, showing off to people (lack of genuine motivation).
summaryEn: The root represents sluggish laziness (Kasal), describing the heavy, unmotivated movements of hypocrites when performing congregational prayers.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الفتور والتثاقل البدني وغياب الهمة (كسل)، واصفاً تثاقل حركة المنافقين نحو الصلاة رياءً بلا رغبة حقيقية (قاموا كسالى).
rawText: أَكْسَلَ عَنْهَا 4 signifies أَوْلَجَ وَلَمْ يُنْزِلْ; [Inivit sed non emisit;] (IAar, in TA, art. فهر;) [i. e., أَوْلَجَ ثُمَّ تَرَكَهَا وَلَمْ يُنْزِلْ]. كَسَّلَهُ 2 , inf. n. تَكْسِيلٌ, said of satiety, It rendered him heavy, sluggish, lazy, indolent, or torpid. (TA.) كَسْلَانٌ Heavy, sluggish, lazy, indolent, torpid. (K.) مَكْسَلَةٌ : see an ex. voce كِطَّةٌ.
